zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Fitz Randolph於1244年出生在英國越可爾米德勒哈姆時，她的父親Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham27歲，母親Anastasia de Percy28歲。她於1260年與Robert de Neville在英國英格蘭諾福爾可亞爾姆斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1320年4月1日在英國英格蘭越可爾可福哈姆享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾可福哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Fitz Randolph于1244年出生在英国越可爾米德勒哈姆时，她的父亲Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham27岁，母亲Anastasia de Percy28岁。她于1260年与Robert de Neville在英国英格兰諾福爾可亞爾姆斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1320年4月1日在英国英格兰越可爾可福哈姆享年76岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾可福哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Randolph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rand
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Rand, a pet form of Randolf. Compare Randolph , Randall , Randy , and see also Rance .
English: habitational name from Rand (Lincolnshire), Rand Grange (North Yorkshire), or Raunds (Northamptonshire), all of which derive from Old English rand ‘edge, border, rim’.
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names formed with rand ‘rim (of a shield)’. Compare 1 above.

variants.name: Randerson
meanings: English (West and East Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Randolf (see Randolph ) + -son. In East Anglia the name may also have been influenced by a form of the now rare or extinct Rendlesham, itself from the place of that name in Suffolk. The placename probably derives from an Old English personal name Rendel (genitive Rendeles) + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.

variants.name: Randolf
meanings: English and German: variant of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randall
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Marie. See Mary .

variants.name: Moira
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Máire , now used throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Fitz Randolph was born in 1244, in Middleham, Yorkshire, England, her father, Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham, was 27 and her mother, Anastasia de Percy, was 28. She married Robert de Neville in 1260, in Yarmouth, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 1 April 1320, in Coverham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Coverham, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Fitz Randolph est née en 1244, à Middleham, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Anastasia de Percy, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Neville en 1260, à Yarmouth, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 avril 1320, à Coverham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Coverham, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Fitz Randolphが1244年にイギリスヨークシュアイアミッドレハムで生まれた時，父Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middlehamは27歳，母Anastasia de Percyは28歳であった。1260年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヤーモウスでRobert de Nevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1320年4月1日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアコバーハムで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアコバーハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Fitz Randolph(이)가 1244년 영국 요르크실르 미들레함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anastasia de Percy(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 야모우스에서 Robert de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1320년 4월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 커버함에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 커버함에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Robert de Neville em 1260, em Yarmouth, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Fitz Randolph родилась в 1244 г. в местности миддлэам, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Anastasia de Percy, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Neville в 1260 г. в местности ярмоут, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 1 апреля 1320 г. в местности ковирам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности ковирам, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Fitz Randolph nació en 1244, en Middleham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph FitzRandolph Lord Middleham, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Anastasia de Percy, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Robert de Neville en 1260, en Yarmouth, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 1 de abril de 1320, en Coverham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Coverham, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
